stories.author: Judd Legum
stories.title: These consumer brands are bankrolling the biggest ICE supporters in Congress
stories.text: During the operation, there have been 28 incidents where immigration agents shot at people, resulting in eight deaths, according to an analysis by The Trace . On July 13, an ICE officer fatally shot Joan Sebastian Durán Guerrero , a 25-year-old delivery driver from Colombia authorized to work in the US. On July 7, an ICE officer shot and killed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo , a father of three who worked in the US for more than three decades. According to federal courts, ICE agents have repeatedly violated the rights of citizens , stopping people for “looking Mexican,” conducting warrantless raids, and ignoring due process . Peaceful protestors and journalists have been targeted with chemical agents , rubber bullets, and flash grenades. Since January 2025, at least 52 people have died in ICE custody from a variety of causes, including medical neglect and homicide. While this dragnet reflects the priorities of Trump and his top advisors, it cannot persist in a vacuum. It requires broader political and economic support. The political support for ICE has come from Congress, which has both massively increased ICE’s funding and repeatedly failed to rein in its excesses. Popular Information ide

stories.author: The Lever team
stories.title: Lever Weekly: Ballot Battles And Blockbusters
stories.text: This is Lever Weekly , a recap of our work from the past week. If you only read one email from us all week, this should be it. The Lever holds the powerful accountable through reader-supported investigative journalism that corporate media will not undertake. Join our mission , and you’ll get great perks in return. Join The Fight Here’s what The Lever published this week: LEVER DEEP DIVE OF THE WEEK Democrats’ Middle Lane Is Collapsing. As Abdul El-Sayed surges, Mallory McMorrow drops out, and Haley Stevens digs in, Michigan is revealing a national realignment inside Democratic politics. LEVER SCOOPS OF THE WEEK The Musk Machine Comes For The Midterms. The law firms that helped Elon Musk gut Delaware’s shareholder protections are now spending big to save the Democratic lawmakers who made it possible. Democrats Fail Because They Won’t Name The Villains. Democrats keep promising to fix the country’s problems. But many refuse to name the corporate interests standing in the way. Maine’s Insider Primary Begins. With Graham Platner out, the battle for Maine's Senate seat just moved behind closed doors. Paramount’s Plot Twist. The Warner Bros.-Paramount merger championed by the Trump admin
